# @joakimbugge/nest-typeorm-seeder

> NestJS module for `@joakimbugge/typeorm-seeder`. Runs your `@Seeder` classes automatically on application bootstrap — once per seeder by default, tracked in a database table so watch-mode restarts do not re-seed. For simple cases a `run` callback lets you seed without any class boilerplate.

This package handles NestJS integration only. The seeding itself — `@Seed()`, `@Seeder`, `seed()`, entity factories — is defined in `@joakimbugge/typeorm-seeder`. Read that package's documentation first.

Full documentation: https://joakimbugge.github.io/seeders/nest/

## Installation

```bash
npm install @joakimbugge/nest-typeorm-seeder @joakimbugge/typeorm-seeder
```

Peer dependencies (`@nestjs/common`, `@nestjs/core`, `typeorm`, `reflect-metadata`) are required but are already present in any NestJS + TypeORM project.

## Basic usage

Import `SeederModule` in your root module. It auto-detects the `DataSource` registered by `TypeOrmModule` — no extra wiring needed:

```ts
import { Module } from '@nestjs/common'
import { TypeOrmModule } from '@nestjs/typeorm'
import { SeederModule } from '@joakimbugge/nest-typeorm-seeder'
import { UserSeeder } from './seeders/UserSeeder.js'

@Module({
  imports: [
    TypeOrmModule.forRoot({ ... }),
    SeederModule.forRoot({ seeders: [UserSeeder] }),
  ],
})
export class AppModule {}
```

Seeders with `@Seeder({ dependencies: [...] })` are topologically sorted and run in the correct order automatically.

`seeders` accepts constructors and glob patterns:

```ts
SeederModule.forRoot({ seeders: [UserSeeder, 'dist/seeders/**/*.js'] })
// TypeScript source files work too when running via ts-node:
SeederModule.forRoot({ seeders: ['src/seeders/**/*.ts'] })
```

## Providing a DataSource explicitly

```ts
SeederModule.forRoot({
  seeders: [UserSeeder],
  dataSource: myDataSource,  // must already be initialized
})
```

## Async configuration

Use `forRootAsync` to resolve options from the DI container:

```ts
import { ConfigService } from '@nestjs/config'

SeederModule.forRootAsync({
  imports: [ConfigModule],
  inject: [ConfigService],
  useFactory: (config: ConfigService) => ({
    seeders: [UserSeeder],
    enabled: config.get('SEED') === 'true',
  }),
})
```

## Run once

By default (`runOnce: true`) each seeder is recorded in a `seeders` table on first run and skipped on subsequent boots. This works like TypeORM migrations.

| `dropSchema` | `runOnce` | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| `true` | `true` | Tracking table dropped → all seeders run every boot |
| `true` | `false` | Always runs |
| `false` | `true` | Already-run seeders are skipped |
| `false` | `false` | Always runs |

`dropSchema: true` is typical in development. `runOnce: true` with a persistent schema is right for staging and production.

### Evolving seed data

Treat seeders like migrations — once one has run in a persistent environment, leave it alone and create a new seeder:

```ts
@Seeder()
class UserSeeder { ... }                              // already ran, do not edit

@Seeder({ dependencies: [UserSeeder] })
class UserSeederV2 { ... }                            // adds more data on next boot
```

### Re-seeding

Force a seeder to run again with `seed:untrack`:

```bash
npx @joakimbugge/typeorm-seeder seed:untrack UserSeeder -d ./dist/datasource.js
```

Or delete directly from the database:

```sql
DELETE FROM "seeders" WHERE name = 'UserSeeder';
DELETE FROM "seeders";  -- reset all
```

## Feature modules

Register seeders alongside the entities they belong to instead of centralising everything in the root module:

```ts
// UserModule.ts
@Module({
  imports: [
    TypeOrmModule.forFeature([User]),
    SeederModule.forFeature([UserSeeder]),
  ],
})
export class UserModule {}

// AppModule.ts
@Module({
  imports: [
    TypeOrmModule.forRoot({ ... }),
    SeederModule,        // or SeederModule.forRoot({ enabled: ... })
    UserModule,
  ],
})
export class AppModule {}
```

`SeederModule` must always be imported at the root — it is what actually runs the seeders collected from `forFeature()`. All seeders from `forRoot` and `forFeature` are merged into a single list before execution.

Cross-module seeder dependencies work without extra wiring:

```ts
@Seeder({ dependencies: [TenantSeeder] })  // TenantSeeder lives in TenantModule
export class UserSeeder implements SeederInterface { ... }
```

## Seed scripts (run callback)

For simple cases that don't need named, tracked seeders:

```ts
SeederModule.forRoot({
  async run({ dataSource }) {
    await seed(User).saveMany(10, { dataSource })
  },
})
```

`run` always executes on every boot — no run-once tracking applies. `runOnce` is not accepted when `seeders` is omitted.

Use both together — seeders run first, then `run`:

```ts
SeederModule.forRoot({
  seeders: [UserSeeder],
  async run({ dataSource }) {
    // UserSeeder has already run at this point
    await seed(AdminUser).save({ dataSource })
  },
})
```
